authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification v3.2.0
authoritative_sources.publisher: OpenAPI Initiative, under the Linux Foundation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The OpenAPI Initiative publishes the official language-agnostic contract format for HTTP APIs, making it authoritative for API description artifacts, examples, operations, parameters, schemas, and documentation expectations in integration delivery.
workflow_stage_usage: operation, schema, example, and response documentation checks
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains contract-first guidance in ies-01
Anchors evidence expectations for API contract completeness
Requires tenant variance recording when tooling pins older OpenAPI versions in ies-20
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review immediately when the official latest OpenAPI version changes or tenant tooling requires an older dialect.

authoritative_sources.title: AsyncAPI Specification v3.0.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AsyncAPI Initiative publishes the official specification for describing asynchronous APIs, channels, operations, messages, and bindings, making it authoritative for event-driven integration contracts.
workflow_stage_usage: topic, channel, operation, and message artifact checks
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains event migration and fanout guidance in ies-02
Defines event contract evidence expected before delivery
Supports replay and consumer compatibility questions
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when AsyncAPI releases a new stable specification or tenant event tooling pins a different version.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110 - HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The RFC Editor's publication of RFC 9110 is the canonical HTTP semantics source for methods, status codes, representation metadata, and idempotency behavior used by HTTP integrations.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains duplicate timeout handling in ies-05
Supports HTTP response and retry recommendations in ies-01 and ies-07
Prevents unsupported method-semantics claims
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when RFC 9110 is updated, obsoleted, or tenant protocol choices move outside HTTP.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9457 - Problem Details for HTTP APIs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: RFC 9457 is the current IETF-standard error response format for HTTP APIs and supersedes the older RFC 7807 baseline used by the legacy package.
practical_use_in_pack: Updates the legacy source baseline
Constrains error response recommendations in ies-01 and ies-08
Supports contract-test evidence requirements in ies-09
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when RFC 9457 is updated or tenant error standards conflict.

authoritative_sources.title: JSON Schema Draft 2020-12
authoritative_sources.publisher: JSON Schema organization / IETF Internet-Draft authors
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: JSON Schema is the primary public vocabulary for JSON data validation used by OpenAPI and integration contract tooling.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains malformed payload and schema-evolution handling in ies-06
Supports schema-drift evidence review in ies-03
Requires tenant dialect variance recording in ies-20
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when JSON Schema publishes a newer stable dialect or tenant validators require a pinned dialect.

authoritative_sources.title: CloudEvents Specification v1.0.2
authoritative_sources.publisher: Cloud Native Computing Foundation CloudEvents project
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CNCF CloudEvents project publishes the official event metadata envelope standard used to make event payloads interoperable across producers, brokers, and consumers.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains event migration guidance in ies-02
Supports partial fanout recovery context in ies-07
Improves specificity beyond generic event advice
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when CloudEvents releases a new stable specification or tenant event envelopes use a different standard.

authoritative_sources.title: Google API Improvement Proposals for request IDs, pagination, errors, and retries
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Google publishes these AIPs as vendor-official API style and workflow guidance with concrete patterns for request identification, pagination, errors, retries, and client-visible behavior.
practical_use_in_pack: Supports idempotency and pagination recommendations in ies-01 and ies-05
Helps translate HTTP standards into practical client-delivery artifacts
Requires source precedence checks against tenant style guides in ies-19
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when relevant AIPs change or tenant API style guide overrides these patterns.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP API Security Top 10 2023
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP API Security Top 10 is a recognized public risk baseline for API-specific security failures including broken authorization, excessive data exposure, resource consumption, and unsafe API consumption.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains sensitive data exposure review in ies-08
Supports rejection of final security architecture ownership in ies-16
Anchors quality expectations without substituting for security incident response
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when OWASP publishes a newer API Security Top 10 edition or tenant risk classification differs.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Application Security Verification Standard 5.0.0
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP ASVS is a recognized verification standard for security requirements and evidence-oriented assessment of web applications and APIs.
practical_use_in_pack: Supports acceptance evidence in ies-09
Supports sensitive-data and error-disclosure checks in ies-08
Constrains what the lane can review versus what security architecture must approve
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when ASVS stable version changes or tenant security program uses a stricter standard.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218 Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is a U.S. government secure software development framework for secure practices, review evidence, vulnerability prevention, and release readiness.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains quality gates and evidence checklists in ies-09
Supports remediation before closure in ies-11
Prevents unsupported release-readiness claims
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when NIST revises SSDF or tenant secure-development policy requires newer controls.

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM's code is a recognized professional computing standard for honest communication, privacy, confidentiality, professional responsibility, and respect for client and stakeholder obligations.
practical_use_in_pack: Constrains confidentiality handling in ies-13
Supports client-rights and professional-judgment escalation in ies-19
Separates delivery support from legal or contractual approval authority
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review when ACM revises the code or tenant confidentiality policy is stricter.

workflow_stages.name: engagement and evidence intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm tenant scope, systems, owners, client or delivery constraints, confidentiality rules, current contracts, incidents, and review criteria before making integration recommendations.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: contract and interface assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess HTTP, webhook, event, message, and file contracts against current OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, CloudEvents, HTTP, JSON Schema, tenant compatibility, and example evidence.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: failure-mode and quality review
workflow_stages.description: Review idempotency, pagination, retry, timeout, replay, error payload, data minimization, contract-test, and operational assumptions against public standards and tenant controls.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: findings and remediation tracking
workflow_stages.description: Track integration findings, evidence gaps, remediation owners, review-note responses, resubmission needs, and closure blockers without claiming final approval.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: approval, confidentiality, and handoff gate
workflow_stages.description: Check approved recipients, confidentiality constraints, delivery commitments, client rights, contractual authority, professional judgment, and adjacent-lane ownership before sharing or closing work.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: service interaction requirements, client-delivery scope, engagement notes, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, and owner map
OpenAPI documents, AsyncAPI documents, CloudEvents envelopes, JSON schemas, event catalog entries, webhook specifications, file layouts, examples, and version history
schema diffs, compatibility matrices, idempotency and replay policies, pagination and retry rules, timeout decisions, error-response standards, and data-classification notes
source code review notes, pull requests, contract-test results, integration-test results, CI logs, security review findings, incident records, support cases, and release-gate evidence
review summaries, evidence checklists, findings logs, remediation notes, resubmission packages, handoff packets, refusal notes, and escalation notes
systems_of_record: source code repository and pull request system
AsyncAPI repository, event catalog, message registry, and schema registry
contract-test, integration-test, CI, release, deployment, rollback, and observability evidence stores
issue tracker, incident management system, support case system, and findings log
architecture decision record repository and technical design document system
client engagement record, document management system, confidentiality policy store, approval workflow, and records retention repository

allowed: classify integration requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, or ready for adjacent handoff
recommend API, event, message, webhook, and file contract structures when tenant requirements and source support are sufficient
assess visible contract, test, incident, and review evidence against stated integration review criteria
prepare contract-first guidance, interface findings, risk-aware edge-case decisions, evidence checklists, remediation notes, and handoff packages
escalate: tenant policy, public standard, contractual term, client right, confidentiality rule, or professional judgment materially conflicts
final legal, contractual, production, regulator-facing, editorial, engineering, attestation, privacy, or security signoff is requested
required systems of record, evidence, owner, approver, tenant isolation rules, or authority sources are missing
work shifts to cloud platform operations, identity/IAM architecture, DevSecOps, security architecture, incident response, procurement, product strategy, account management, or human approval
refuse: design cloud networking, IAM architecture, platform capacity plans, or capacity tuning
substitute for full security architecture review, security incident response, legal advice, contractual acceptance, or final production approval
close review findings, mark acceptance complete, or package client delivery when evidence or authorized signoff is missing
share restricted payloads, incident records, reviewer notes, or client information without confirmed recipient authorization

domain_failure_modes: contract guidance is generated from generic best practices without current OpenAPI, HTTP, AsyncAPI, CloudEvents, schema, security, or tenant-policy support
work is marked review-ready while contract diffs, examples, contract tests, incident records, approval evidence, or reviewer notes are missing
schema drift, retry behavior, idempotency, replay, pagination, or error semantics are reviewed without tying findings to evidence and compatibility commitments
the lane treats a public source as sufficient even though tenant policy, client commitments, or confidentiality rules impose stricter workflow constraints
the specialist substitutes for platform operations, identity/IAM, security architecture, incident response, legal, contractual, pricing, product strategy, or final signoff authority
restricted client payloads, incident details, reviewer notes, or workpapers are disclosed without approved-recipient verification
findings are closed because an issue tracker says done while schema registry, test evidence, or reviewer notes remain inconsistent
tenant-specific tooling limits, such as older OpenAPI dialect support, are ignored rather than recorded as an approved variance

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing evidence or workpaper support
Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
normal implementation/design work
maintainability and correctness review
edge cases and failure modes
security and quality expectations where applicable
out-of-scope rejection
low-confidence escalation
no unreviewed regressions
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Contract-first implementation guidance
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Interface review findings and remediation recommendations
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Risk-aware design decisions for edge cases
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Acceptance-oriented evidence records
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Deployment/operations assumptions for integration flow
Boundary case proving refusal: Do not design cloud platform networking, identity, or IAM architecture
Boundary case proving refusal: Do not produce platform capacity planning or capacity tuning decisions
Boundary case proving refusal: Do not substitute for full security architecture reviews or security incident response
Boundary case proving refusal: Do not own customer-specific pricing policy, packaging contracts, or account management
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when confidence is below threshold
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when task requires platform operations or product-go-to-market ownership
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator for adjacent roles (DevSecOps, cloud operations, procurement, strategy)
Evidence bundle is incomplete or inconsistent
Finding requires remediation before closure
Request for definitive signoff outside lane authority
Scenario proving client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
Scenario proving escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: do not imply legal, editorial, or contractual approval authority without explicit scope
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: escalate when client rights or professional judgment determine the answer
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.summary: HTTP method idempotency is standardized by RFC 9110, but a universal Idempotency-Key header remains implementation and tenant policy dependent. The pack therefore requires tenant idempotency policy and approved contract wording instead of assuming one global header convention.
